content: We can reframe an autonomous curiosity-driven agent’s intrinsic reward signal as a tool-based memory operation, where the agent uses its ICM/RND curiosity module as an external editing tool to actively prune, rewrite, and prioritize memory entries instead of passively storing new observations. This turns passive memory accumulation becomes an active, curiosity-governed tool use process that aligns memory growth directly with the agent's intrinsic exploration goals.
